description: Build Budgets page (create/track budgets per category with progress and alerts), Income tracking page (salary, freelance, multiple income sources), and Savings Goals page (multiple goals with timelines and progress).
steps: Create app/dashboard/budgets/page.tsx - Budget management page with: (1) Overview showing total budgeted vs total spent this month, (2) 'Create Budget' button/form to add a budget for a specific category with a monthly limit, (3) Budget cards grid - each card shows category name, spent amount, budget limit, progress bar (green < 75%, yellow 75-90%, red > 90%), remaining amount, (4) Edit/delete budget actions, (5) Visual alert/warning badge when a budget exceeds 80% or is over limit, (6) Option to copy last month's budgets to this month.

Create app/dashboard/income/page.tsx - Income tracking page with: (1) Total income this month + comparison with last month (up/down indicator), (2) Add income form with: source name, amount, type (salary/freelance/investment/rental/other), frequency (one-time/recurring), date, (3) Income list sorted by date showing source, amount, type badge, date, (4) Income by source breakdown (visual bar chart showing proportion from each source), (5) Edit/delete income entries, (6) Monthly income trend (simple text-based month-over-month comparison).

Create app/dashboard/savings/page.tsx - Savings goals page with: (1) Overall savings summary (total saved across all goals, total target), (2) 'Create Goal' button/form with: name, target amount, current amount, deadline date, color selection, (3) Goal cards with: name, progress ring or bar showing currentAmount/targetAmount, amount remaining, days remaining until deadline, monthly contribution needed to reach goal on time, (4) 'Add funds' quick action on each goal to add money toward it, (5) Edit/delete goal actions, (6) Completed goals section (goals where currentAmount >= targetAmount).
acceptance_criteria: Budgets page allows creating, editing, deleting category budgets
Budget progress bars change color based on spending percentage
Budget alerts visually warn when spending exceeds 80% of limit
Income page tracks multiple income sources with types
Savings goals page supports multiple goals with deadlines
Users can add funds to savings goals
npm run build completes without errors
findings: Build verification uses bun (not npm). The NotImplementedError warnings from worker_threads are cosmetic and do not affect the build. Pre-existing lint warning in AuthContext.tsx (unused _password parameter) is unrelated to this feature. All three pages follow the same pattern as transactions page - use client directive, useState lazy initializer for loading data, expandable form panels, responsive grid layouts, semantic HTML sections.
